title: Oscar's Garden Adventure
synopsis: Oscar feels worried about starting his new nature school, but with help from his teacher Ms. Elena and new friends, he discovers the joy of gardening and finds his courage to try new things.

pages.text: Oscar held his daddy's hand tightly. Today was his first day at nature school. His tummy felt funny and scared. 'What if I don't like it?' Oscar whispered.

illustration.description: A small boy with curly brown hair and brown skin stands next to his tall father with glasses and a beard. Oscar looks worried, clutching his father's hand. They stand in front of a colorful school building with garden beds full of bright flowers. The sky is cheerful blue with puffy white clouds.
illustration.alt: Oscar holding his father's hand, looking worried in front of his new nature school

pages.text: Ms. Elena, his teacher, had kind eyes and a warm smile. She wore a sun hat and gardening gloves. 'Hello, Oscar!' she said happily. 'We're going to have so much fun today!'

illustration.description: A friendly teacher with long black hair wearing a wide-brimmed yellow sun hat and green gardening apron waves cheerfully. She has olive skin and sparkling brown eyes. Behind her are raised garden beds with colorful vegetables and a small greenhouse. The scene feels warm and welcoming.
illustration.alt: Ms. Elena the teacher welcoming Oscar with a bright smile

pages.text: Oscar felt a little bit brave when he saw other children playing near the garden. A girl with red braids waved at him. A boy with dark skin was digging in the dirt and laughing.

illustration.description: Several diverse children playing in a school garden. A girl with red braids and freckles waves happily. A boy with dark skin and a big smile digs with a small shovel. Another child with Asian features waters plants with a small watering can. The garden is full of green plants and bright flowers.
illustration.alt: Diverse children playing happily in the school garden

pages.text: Ms. Elena showed Oscar the empty garden bed. 'This needs seeds!' she said. But Oscar felt scared to touch the dirt. It looked messy and weird. His hands stayed clean and still.

illustration.description: Oscar stands hesitantly next to an empty rectangular garden bed filled with dark brown soil. Ms. Elena kneels beside it holding colorful seed packets. Oscar has his hands behind his back, looking uncertain. The garden bed is surrounded by blooming plants and vegetables in other beds.
illustration.alt: Oscar looking scared at the empty garden bed while Ms. Elena holds seeds

pages.text: The other children were planting seeds and getting dirty. They looked so happy! Oscar wanted to join them, but he felt too scared. What if he did it wrong?

illustration.description: Happy children with dirty hands plant seeds in their garden beds. The girl with red braids has soil on her cheeks but is smiling widely. The boy is carefully placing seeds in rows, concentrating happily. Oscar watches from the side, looking sad and left out.
illustration.alt: Happy children gardening while Oscar watches sadly from the side

pages.text: Ms. Elena sat next to Oscar. 'It's okay to feel scared,' she said gently. 'Plants need gentle hands like yours. Would you like to try with me?' Oscar nodded slowly.

illustration.description: Ms. Elena sits cross-legged on the grass next to Oscar, speaking to him with a kind, patient expression. Oscar looks up at her with trust beginning to show in his eyes. Small butterflies flutter around nearby flowers, and the afternoon sun casts a warm golden glow over the garden scene.
illustration.alt: Ms. Elena comforting Oscar in the garden

pages.text: Oscar touched the soft dirt with one finger. It felt cool and nice! Ms. Elena helped him make a small hole. Together, they dropped in a tiny sunflower seed. Oscar felt proud and happy!

illustration.description: Oscar's small hand carefully placing a tiny black sunflower seed into a hole in the dark soil. Ms. Elena's hand guides his gently. Oscar's face shows wonder and growing confidence. The soil looks rich and welcoming, with other planted areas visible in the background showing tiny green sprouts.
illustration.alt: Oscar planting his first seed with Ms. Elena's help

pages.text: Soon Oscar was digging and planting with his new friends. His hands got dirty, but he didn't mind! 'I can't wait to see my sunflower grow!' he said, feeling brave and excited about tomorrow.

illustration.description: Oscar now has dirt on his hands and clothes, laughing joyfully as he plants seeds alongside his new friends. The red-haired girl and the other boy work nearby, all chatting and smiling. Small watering cans and tools are scattered around. The garden looks alive with potential, and Oscar's face beams with happiness and confidence.
illustration.alt: Oscar happily gardening with his new friends, looking confident and joyful

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Oscar feel at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: Oscar felt scared and worried about starting his new nature school. His tummy felt funny and he whispered 'What if I don't like it?'

questions.question: What did Oscar plant in the garden?
questions.explanation: Oscar planted a tiny sunflower seed with Ms. Elena's help. He said 'I can't wait to see my sunflower grow!'

questions.question: Who helped Oscar feel brave?
questions.explanation: Ms. Elena helped Oscar feel brave by sitting with him, telling him it's okay to feel scared, and helping him plant his first seed.

questions.question: How did Oscar feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: At the end, Oscar felt brave and excited about tomorrow. He was laughing and playing with his new friends in the garden.

pages.title: Complete Oscar's Day
pages.text: Help finish Oscar's story! Choose the best words to complete each sentence.

sentences.text: Oscar felt _____ about his first day at nature school.
Oscar planted a _____ seed in the garden.

pages.title: What Happens Next?
pages.text: Put Oscar's feelings in the right order from the beginning to the end of the story.

sentences.text: Then, he felt _____ to try something new.
